title: Bacteriophage and Phenotypic Variation in Pseudomonas aeruginosa Biofilm Development
summary: The paper describes Pf4 as a filamentous phage (Pf1-like) that emerges from Pseudomonas aeruginosa biofilms and causes small-colony variants (SCVs). Pf1 is referenced as a related filamentous phage. No detailed virion dimensions or structural proteins are provided, but immunoelectron microscopy shows Pf4 filaments form dense, interwoven latticeworks on SCV cell surfaces.

Pf4.plaque_morphology: capable of forming plaques on the P. aeruginosa host strain
Pf4.structural_proteins: CoaB (PA0723; the major coat protein of the filamentous phage virion)

extra.description: filaments on the cell surface, often tightly interwoven into complex latticeworks surrounding the cells
extra.immunogold_labeling: rabbit anti-Pf4 antiserum used with 12-nm-diameter gold particles
extra.replicative_form: double-stranded circular copies of the genome known as replicative forms
extra.superinfective: able to form plaques on the lysogenic P. aeruginosa strain

Pf1.structural_proteins: CoaB (PA0723; the major coat protein of the filamentous phage virion)
